item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is good, it's wholesome and it's a cool article. It is short but I think that adds to the charm of the article, it is a nice break from the other articles and is nice that a scp is doing good in the world without foundation intervention

Just looked at the header (the pictures of those symbol things at the top) and it's pretty distorted on my laptop, will check if it's a hardware bug or not

+1

item.title: TAKE MY +1
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I'm who traslated the article to the Spanish Branch, so, hello awa

Now, speaking about the article, the anomaly is… Poor for say something. But is not that what make it special, what do it special is all the artistic meaning. Just take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Yes

This is why I am a fan of Esoteric Containment Classes. Just a small change to the normal SCP Rules can make for some really interesting (and in this case lovely) situations. Nice work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me all happy inside :)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. Started crying while reading this.

item.title: Cool Reference
item.content:encoded:
The title is a nice reference for those who get it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a wonderful little part of kindness, and I think that’s something we all need right now. Just a little bit of hope to light the way.

Have an upvote from me, because things like this are important to so many people these days.

item.title: thank you
item.content:encoded:
i am having a few tears coming out of my eyes. thank you for writing this.
as someone who's been through that sort of thing. i will tell you that this one. this one actually seams likely. for some reason i feel i've seen this, or at least something similar to this in memories of an incident that happened to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. Third one really hit me with the feels and made me tear up a bit. Haha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easy +1. So glad I decided to click on this random scip while browsing Series 6. The message about the dress drew me in, and the rest absolutely delivered. Honestly with the image (Which startled me, honestly, lol), I thought I was in for a terrifying, malicious anomaly, but I'm so glad I was wrong. The real strength of this article, I think, is how… Human? It is? Like, there's something really humbling about the good deeds being described here, something that cuts to the heart of what it means to be a human desperately trying to be kind. Awesome work.

item.title: Edit
item.content:encoded: In your page source, you had a horizontal line under your image component. This showed up as a second horizontal line under your acs component which got cut off by the image. I removed it, but feel free to undo my changes if you wanted it there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. makes me feel warm and fuzzy
Footnote 2 says SCP-5669; from the message, I'm guessing it's meant to be 5699?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite good for me +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally like the scp buuuuutttt….

The author page module is borked

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me tear a bit, I gotta admit. I also think that what makes the message so powerful is that this skip is here for make those who are at their lowest that no good deed goes unnoticed.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn this post hits hard, I wish 5699 were with me

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoting, but you've definitely done something right here. The notes create a very solid atmosphere, but then you don't end up doing much with it. The final note doesn't do anything for me, nor do the notes achieve the escalation I think you were looking for. The end result doesn't have a strong emotional impact on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1. I thought it was sappy. Vague descriptions of acts of kindness from a completely unknown person to another don't actually have any impact for me.

One less subjective criticism is the lack of clinical tone. I'd imagine something like this would be recorded in a log, probably table format, with dates, locations, subject, etc…

Maybe relegate all but one note to a collapsible log, and give it more details, and then let the reader use their imagination for the rest. As it stands, this feels like reading through the greeting cards section at a dollar store.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Apparently it's from Meshes of the Afternoon, an experimental film directed by Maya Deren and Alexander Hammid. According to Wikimedia Commons, this film was "originally published in 1943 without a copyright notice and thus entered the public domain upon publication."

Edit: Sorry! My reply overlapped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meshes of the Afternoon. Classic Surrealist film. Highly recommended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hit like a damn freight train. +1.

Unrelated note, where is that picture from? It looks like something out of an old art house movie and I'm curious what it was supposed to be in its original context

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gave me that tingly hairs-standing-on-end feeling in a good way. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meshes of the Afternoon is one of my favorite surrealist films! I love this article too - it plays on emotions in a way that doesn't feel objectifying towards any specific person or group. Hearty +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha dress go spinny

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Short and sweet. It stays around just for as long as it needs to to make its tone and point known. My only note is a grammatical error in the containment procedures. If I'm not mistaken it should be written as 'has been suspended' instead of 'had been suspended', unless containment is no longer suspended for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, did this hit so hard for me. I think this really encompasses everything I wish something like SCP-4999 could have done a little bit better, what with showcasing the emotional states and situations people visited by the anomaly were going through. Instead of getting muddled and disjointed with showing very disparate situations, you managed to create a palatable oeuvre of kindness and warmth in this piece. So emotional, so bittersweet, so absolutely lovely. Hard upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE S P I N

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where are the invisible ninjas cutting onions? Where are they?

Jokes aside, not a lot of things can make me cry. I can't even remember the last time anything on this site has. But goddamn if this didn't make me misty-eyed. I echo Nagiro's sentiment of there being some clunky language here and there but this still gets a definite +1 from me.

Edit: I just noticed that the number is wrong here. In the article you call it 5669, but this is placed in 5699 on the mainlist. You might want to fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great concept, solid execution.

Echoing the points about tone and flow, but nothing some minor edits can't smooth out.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You bought me a dress and helped me with the straps. You told me I was pretty and loved, and that the girl who had been inside of me the whole time was finally freed.

show
hide

YES +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's still a bit clunky in terms of clinical tone and some formatting here and there, but damn if that final note doesn't hit hard.

item.title: Short, simple, and sweet
item.content:encoded:
It's short, but tells all it needs to tell.
It's a very interesting concept that has emotion to it.
I liked it :) Very enjoyable +1
